title: Blechnum procerum
intro: Blechnum procerum, also known as the Blechnum fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum procerum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Blechnum procerum is commonly used to make teas and infusions, which can also be dried and used in capsule or tincture form.
taxonomy: Blechnum procerum is classified as a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Blechnum and is a type of tree fern. The Blechnum procerum is native to New Zealand and is known for its unique and adaptable nature. This species of fern is often found in shaded areas and is commonly used in landscaping and conservation efforts. Blechnum procerum is also known for its ability to thrive in a variety of environments.
